meta.title: FlowCraft | Find Your Focus with ADHD
meta.description: FlowCraft is the ongoing practice of designing, testing, and refining the conditions for your own focus. Join the waitlist for group coaching and exclusive app access.

hero.headline: No one taught you how to find your focus.
hero.lead: Every productivity system you've tried promises to get you into flow consistently. And none of them reliably do. It's time to stop fixing yourself and start designing for your brain.
hero.subtext: Get notified about the next group coaching session + exclusive app access.

quote.text: “You try the system, it works for a few days, maybe a week, and then it falls apart. Is it the system? Or is it me?”

story1.heading: I didn’t have a name for it yet.
story1.paragraph1: If you had walked past the right grassy area in community college, you might have spotted me, backpack on the ground, juggling balls in the air, completely in my own world.
story1.paragraph2: I thought I was just being weird. But if I didn’t move my body and do something that demanded my full attention, I couldn’t sit still and focus when I got back to class.
story1.paragraph3: I was spiking my dopamine. Moving my body boosts the neurotransmitters my ADHD brain was starving for. And juggling? It demands complete attention. There is no room for mental noise when you’re trying to keep three balls in the air.
story1.pullQuote: “I was already doing FlowCraft. I just didn’t have a name for it yet.”

story2.heading: It wasn’t luck. It was a structure.
story2.paragraph1: Working the afternoon shift at a Starbucks in Tucson, a customer handed me a book: Flow by Mihaly Csikszentmihalyi.
story2.paragraph2: He explained that there is a specific state of consciousness where you become completely absorbed in what you’re doing. Time distorts. Self-consciousness disappears.
story2.paragraph3: It’s not random. It has identifiable conditions. You can learn to create them.
story2.paragraph4: That planted the idea that focus wasn’t something that happened to you; it was something you could learn to find. On purpose.

framework.heading: So, what is FlowCraft?
framework.lead: FlowCraft is the ongoing practice of designing, testing, and refining the conditions for your own focus.

cards.title: 1. Designing
cards.body: Not following. Not copying. Designing. When I rode that unicycle, I was designing a pre-focus ritual for my specific brain. You are not going to find your version of this in a productivity book.

cards.title: 2. Testing
cards.body: FlowCraft is empirical. You form a hypothesis: what if I work in shorter bursts? And then you run the experiment. You’re not failing if it doesn’t work; you’re learning.

cards.title: 3. Refining
cards.body: Most systems assume that once you find the right system, you’re done. That’s not how life works, and it’s not how ADHD works. You refine continuously as your life changes.

loop.heading: The FlowCraft Loop
loop.lead: At the heart of FlowCraft is a simple loop.

steps.title: Observe
steps.body: Pay attention to when you’re focused and when you’re not. Gather data about yourself without judgment.

steps.title: Experiment
steps.body: Deliberately introduce changes. New environments, timing, rituals. Treat it like a hypothesis, not a commitment.

steps.title: Measure
steps.body: Track results and the felt experience. Did it work? Did time disappear? Both tracks of data are real.

steps.title: Adjust
steps.body: Take what you learned and modify. Keep what works. Drop what doesn’t. Then observe again.

app.label: The Tool & The Team
app.heading: You don’t have to do this alone.
app.body: FlowCraft is not a system I can hand you, but I can coach you through the process. I’ve built a webapp to support your practice, and I’m launching a group coaching cohort to guide you through the loop.
checklist: Exclusive access to the FlowCraft Webapp
A community of ADHD knowledge workers

shift.heading: A Shift in the Question
shift.intro: Most of us with ADHD have spent years asking:
shift.quote1: “Why can’t I just do what other people seem to do?”
shift.quote2: “What conditions make it more possible for my brain to function well?”
shift.closing: You’re not measuring yourself against a neurotypical standard. You’re trying to understand a system. Your system.

finalCta.heading: Ready to find your flow?
finalCta.lead: Join the waitlist to be the first to know when coaching enrollment opens.
